stories.headline: Reeves tells Americans Trump’s Iran war is a ‘mistake’
stories.summary: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves has criticized Donald Trump's war on Iran, calling it a "mistake" that has destabilized the global economy and damaged living standards worldwide. Reeves stated that the US made a mistake by ending diplomatic negotiations with Iran and entering into military conflict. She believes this decision has not made the world a safer place. The chancellor's comments mark a fraying of the transatlantic relationship, as she directly criticized Trump's decision to break off diplomatic talks and launch airstrikes against Iran. Reeves' criticism emphasizes the negative impact of the conflict on the global economy and living standards, highlighting the far-reaching consequences of the US actions in the region. Her statements reflect a significant divergence in opinion between the UK and US on the handling of the Iran situation.

stories.headline: Hungary's new leader has vowed to wean his country off Russian energy. It will be a painful transition.
stories.summary: Hungary's incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar has vowed to wean the country off Russian energy by 2035. This move marks a significant shift in Hungary's politics, away from Russia and towards Europe. Magyar's promise comes after winning the April 12 election, where he pledged to revive the country's stagnant economy since 2022 and improve relations with the EU. Under former Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year rule, ties between Brussels and Budapest were tense due to his close ties with Russia. Magyar's plan to eliminate dependence on Russian energy imports is expected to be a painful transition, but it aligns with his goal of bringing Hungary back into the European fold. The new leader's policies are likely to shape not only domestic policy but also Europe's energy ties with Russia. Magyar aims to achieve this goal by 2035.

stories.headline: US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz: What do the numbers show?
stories.summary: The US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has significantly impacted ship traffic, with a 95% decrease since the start of the war. On the first full day of the blockade, at least eight vessels passed through the critical waterway, despite the restrictions. Marine tracking data shows that hundreds of tanker ships were waiting to enter the Strait in early April 2026. A naval blockade requires a formal announcement and defined boundaries. Ship tracking data analysis reveals that some Iran-linked ships continued to pass through the Strait after the blockade began. The drastic reduction in ship traffic highlights the blockade's effectiveness in restricting maritime activity in the region. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway, and the blockade's impact on global trade and oil supplies is being closely monitored.

stories.headline: 'They don't need to agree on everything': Trump's EU envoy plays down spat with Pope Leo
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and Pope Leo XIV are engaged in a war of words, with the President seeking to end Iranian civilization and the Pope advocating for peace. The US envoy to the EU has downplayed the spat, stating that Trump and the Pope "don't have to agree on everything." The disagreement stems from their differing approaches to achieving peace, with Trump considering military action against Iran and the Pope opposing it. The Pope has responded to Trump's comments, saying "Blessed are the peacemakers." The US envoy's comments aim to defend Trump's attacks on the Pope, while also acknowledging their differences in opinion on how to achieve peace. The situation highlights the contrasting views of the two leaders on international relations and conflict resolution.

stories.headline: Adobe Inc Stock (ADBE) Moved Up by 3.55% on Apr 15: What Investors Need To Know
stories.summary: Adobe Inc's stock (ADBE) rose 3.55% on April 15. The company recently launched Firefly, an AI assistant aimed at enhancing creative workflows. This development comes as analysts support Adobe's price hike move, expected to contribute to the company's revenue forecast for fiscal year 2025, which predicts an 8.9% growth rate. Although Adobe's stock previously hit a 52-week low of $233.10 on April 9 due to market pressures, the current increase suggests a positive trend. The company's fiscal year 2024 growth rate was 10.8%, and the new forecast indicates a slightly slower but still steady growth. Investors are monitoring the stock's performance and latest news to inform their trading and investment decisions. Adobe's stock price and latest news are available for analysis, providing vital information for investors.

stories.headline: Google unleashes a native Gemini app for the Mac
stories.summary: Google has released a native Gemini app for Mac, available for download at gemini.google/mac, for macOS versions 15 and up. The app can be activated using the Option + Space keyboard shortcut and features built-in tools for generating content. The Gemini app for Mac is subject to existing controls in the Workspace Admin console, specifically the Generative AI settings. Users can download the app by visiting gemini.google/mac and selecting "Download for Mac." The launch marks the first expansion of Gemini beyond Android and iOS, with Google aiming to build a "truly personal, proactive and powerful desktop assistant." The app is available globally, starting today, and users can find more information on enabling or disabling it in the Help Center.

stories.headline: 20-year-old Enlightenment E16 bug finally gets patched
stories.summary: A 20-year-old bug in Enlightenment E16, a Linux window manager, has been patched by software developer Kamila Szewczyk. The bug, which originated in 2006, was a show-stopping issue with rare occurrences, making it challenging to identify and fix. Despite being an older version, Enlightenment E16 is still used by some, including the editor-in-chief of a blog, who was born in 2004 and uses it daily. The fix was made possible by determining a test case, allowing Szewczyk to identify and resolve the issue. Enlightenment E16 was initially developed in the late 1990s, with its development stopping in 2000, when the focus shifted to creating the Enlightenment Foundation Libraries and rewriting Enlightenment to utilize them, resulting in version 0.17.0 in 2012, which is incompatible with E16.

stories.headline: Best Buy will give you a free LG TV when you buy the B5 OLED at 50% off - seriously
stories.summary: Best Buy is offering a 50% discount on the LG B5 OLED TV, with prices starting at $649.99 for the 48-inch model and $799.99 for the 55-inch model. This discount is also available on Amazon. The LG B5 OLED features excellent picture quality, a 120Hz refresh rate, and four HDMI 2.1 ports. Additionally, when purchasing the B5 OLED at the discounted price, Best Buy is including a free LG TV, which is automatically added to the cart and zeroed out at checkout. The free TV offer is available when buying the B5 OLED at the discounted price, making it a valuable deal for those looking to upgrade their home theater setup. The sale price is the best ever offered for the 55-inch model, making it a significant savings opportunity.

stories.headline: Kerstin Ong breaks national record, Shanti Pereira pulls up at Singapore Open track & field c’ships Ong had paid for three of her HK rivals' accommodation so that they could 'push' her to a better timing.
stories.summary: Kerstin Ong broke the national 100m hurdles record with a time of 13.81 seconds at the Singapore Open. Notably, Ong had paid for the accommodation of three of her Hong Kong rivals to compete against her, aiming to push herself to a better timing. This achievement comes after Ong, along with teammates Shanti Pereira, Laavinia Jaiganth, and Elizabeth-Ann Tan, equalled the Singapore women's 4x100m relay national record at the 2025 Singapore Open Track and Field Championships. Ong's record-breaking performance is part of her goal to qualify for the Asian Games. Meanwhile, Shanti Pereira pulled up during the event, but details of her condition are not specified. Ong's self-funded initiative to invite stronger competitors demonstrates her dedication to improving her performance and pursuing her athletic goals.

stories.headline: Harry Maguire hit with ban and fine after 'f*cking joke' outburst at match officials revealed An independent FA panel has handed Manchester United's Harry Maguire a one-match ban and £30k fine for explicit comments, worsening their defensive crisis.
stories.summary: Manchester United's Harry Maguire has been handed a one-match ban and a £30,000 fine by an independent FA panel for explicit comments made towards match officials. The incident occurred during a game against Bournemouth, where Maguire was sent off for a foul. As he left the field, he allegedly shouted at the officials, saying "You're a joke. You're all a fucking joke." Maguire admitted to the charge and apologized, which was taken into account by the FA, resulting in a reduced ban from the possible two-match suspension. His previous disciplinary record also contributed to the mitigating factors. The ban will worsen Manchester United's defensive crisis. Maguire's admission and apology were considered in the decision, leading to the one-game ban and fine. The FA's written reasons confirmed the details of the incident and the subsequent disciplinary action.

stories.headline: LIV Golf: Sergio Garcia responds to speculation over future of Saudi-backed league amid 'announcement' rumours The future of LIV fuelled by speculation on social media since Tuesday night; Sergio Garcia, speaking ahead of this weeks event in Mexico, says: I cant tell you
stories.summary: Sergio Garcia addressed speculation about the future of LIV Golf, a Saudi-backed league, ahead of its Mexico event. Garcia stated that players were told at the start of the year that the project would run for "many years." He denied having any information about the league's potential demise, citing Yasir Al-Rumayyan's earlier statement of support. Garcia said players will only listen to official announcements from the chairman, dismissing rumors fueled by social media speculation since Tuesday. The Spanish golfer's comments aimed to calm doubts about LIV Golf's future, which have been circulating online. Garcia reiterated that he has not heard anything to suggest the league is on the verge of collapse, and that players are focused on the upcoming tournament in Mexico City.

stories.headline: Maguire corruption calls conceal perfectly valid complaint over ridiculous FA ban Man Utd fans have an axe to grind with the FA over Maguire's ban and while one specific whataboutery is misplaced, further complaints are perfectly reasonable.
stories.summary: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has been handed a 15-month suspended sentence in Greece for a 2020 altercation with police in Mykonos. The incident occurred in August 2020, and Maguire was found guilty by a Greek court. Separately, Maguire has been charged by the Football Association (FA) for improper conduct following his red card in a Premier League match against Bournemouth on March 20. The FA charge is related to his behavior after being sent off in the 78th minute of the game. Maguire's situation has sparked complaints from Manchester United fans, who feel the FA has been unfair in its treatment of the player. While some criticisms may be misplaced, others argue that the FA's handling of the situation is worthy of further scrutiny. Maguire faces potential further punishment from the FA.

stories.headline: Assembly Elections 2026 highlights: PM Modi holds roadshow in Tamil Nadu, says DMK losing 'for sure'
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a roadshow in Nagercoil, Tamil Nadu, in support of the NDA and AIADMK alliance for the upcoming Assembly elections. He stated that the DMK will lose "for sure" as campaigning intensifies ahead of the April 23 voting. The roadshow is part of the BJP's efforts to gain traction in the state. Meanwhile, other key political figures, including Chief Minister MK Stalin, are also mobilizing support through roadshows. The elections are expected to be a multi-cornered contest, with regional and national leaders participating. Other leaders, such as TVK chief Vijay, have also conducted roadshows in Chennai, highlighting the intense voter attention in the state. The elections are scheduled to take place on April 23, with various parties and alliances vying for power in Tamil Nadu.

stories.headline: Judges Decide As Per Law But Are Called Independent Based On Outcomes In High-Stakes Cases: Justice Rajesh Bindal
stories.summary: Retiring Supreme Court judge Justice Rajesh Bindal stated that judges decide cases based on the law, but the judiciary is often criticized depending on the outcome's favorability. He emphasized the importance of upholding the values of justice, equality, and rule of law as enshrined in the Constitution. Justice Bindal also highlighted the judiciary's role in preventing arbitrary exercise of power by the executive and legislature. He noted that a lack of data on arbitration hinders policy formation. Fellow judge Justice Augustine George Masih advised judges to ignore publicity and public outrage, deciding cases strictly by law. Justice Bindal's remarks underscore the challenges faced by the judiciary in high-stakes cases, where outcomes can be heavily scrutinized and influence public perception of judicial independence. His comments come as he concludes his tenure as a Supreme Court judge.

stories.headline: Sabarimala | Visit Of Fertile Women Antithetical To Deity's Identity; They Can Visit Other Ayyappa Temples : TDB To Supreme Court
stories.summary: The Travancore Devaswom Board (TDB) argued in the Supreme Court that the entry of "fertile women" between 10 and 50 years old is antithetical to the deity's identity at Sabarimala temple. The TDB suggested that women in this age group can visit other Ayyappa temples, where they are allowed. This argument is based on the assumption that the presence of fertile women would contradict the celibate vow observed at Sabarimala. The court was also reminded that women are allowed to enter all other temples of Ayyappan, making the exception for Sabarimala unusual. The TDB's argument emphasizes that devotees have ample opportunity to visit Lord Ayyappa in other temples, implying that the restriction at Sabarimala is not a significant impediment to their worship. The Supreme Court is considering the matter, weighing individual conscience and religious freedom.

stories.headline: Omar Abdullah refuses to cut Tricolour ribbon at event, video goes viral
stories.summary: Omar Abdullah, Chief Minister of Jammu and Kashmir, refused to cut a tricolour ribbon at the inaugural ceremony of the "Know Your Artisan" initiative, a government program promoting the region's handicrafts and artistic heritage. The ribbon, designed in the colours of the Indian national flag, was to be cut by Abdullah as the chief guest. However, upon noticing the flag colours, he stopped short and instead untied the ribbon, asking the organisers to fold and keep it. The incident, captured on video, has gone viral on social media. Abdullah's actions were seen as a sign of respect for the Indian national flag, which cannot be cut or damaged according to protocol. The event was part of the "Soulful Kashmir" campaign, aimed at promoting the region's cultural heritage.

stories.headline: Markets show cautious resilience amid volatility, with Bitcoin up about 5% and oil retreating below $100; geopolitics and Iran crypto use shape near-term risk.
stories.summary: Oil prices have fallen below $100 a barrel after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire, while Bitcoin has surged about 5% to break $71,000 in volatile after-hours trading. The ceasefire has eased tensions in the Middle East, leading to a decline in oil prices. Meanwhile, Iran is considering requiring oil tankers to pay Bitcoin tolls to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, potentially creating a new use case for cryptocurrency. Gold prices remain 11% below late-February highs due to forced liquidation during the escalation of tensions. The recent bilateral contact and ceasefire agreement have allowed for sentiment recovery in global capital markets, but risk management remains crucial amid ongoing volatility and potential shifts in market expectations. Traders are advised to maintain disciplined position sizing and respect established technical levels.

stories.headline: The Sensex has surged nearly 9% in April amid hopes for an end to the US-Iran conflict and falling crude oil prices.
stories.summary: The Sensex has surged nearly 9% in April, poised to end its four-month losing streak, driven by improved market sentiment. Hopes for an end to the US-Iran conflict and falling crude oil prices have boosted investor confidence. The Indian stock market rally is attributed to easing Middle East tensions and a significant drop in crude oil prices, with Brent crude falling below $95 per barrel. Key sectors such as IT, financials, metals, and PSU banks have seen significant gains. A stronger rupee has also contributed to the market's upward trend. Despite the gains, experts caution that key risks including geopolitics, Fed policy, and tariffs remain, which could impact the market's performance in the coming days. The Sensex and Nifty indices have surged over 1% in recent trading sessions, reflecting the optimism surrounding potential US-Iran peace talks.

stories.headline: No third headline provided to select from the given list, moving to the next step.
stories.summary: The Washington Post, Al Jazeera, and the New York Post are providing live coverage and the latest headlines on US and international news. The New York Times is also offering live news, investigations, and opinion pieces from over 150 countries. AllSides Headline Roundups are providing balanced news with bias analysis, showcasing top stories from the left, center, and right of the political spectrum. The headlines are rotated to avoid bias, ensuring a comprehensive view of current events. These news outlets are offering a range of perspectives and coverage, including exclusive video footage, photos, and updated maps. Readers can stay informed on the latest developments in politics, business, technology, and more through these reputable sources. Subscription options are available for those looking to stay up-to-date on the latest news.

stories.headline: No fourth headline provided to select from the given list, unable to fulfill the request as only two headlines were provided.
stories.summary: A request for a news summary was made, but only two headlines were provided, and no fourth headline was available to select from the given list. The purpose of a headline is to draw attention to a story, typically written by a copy editor or other editors. ABC News Live offers 24/7 coverage of news headlines and events from around the world, including live news, investigations, and more. The New York Times journalists provide coverage from over 150 countries. A separate topic mentioned a mod lobby list for a game, noting that inactive lobbies disappear from the list and the mod is in beta, with potential bugs. The request for a news summary cannot be fulfilled due to the lack of a fourth headline. The provided information appears to be a mix of news and gaming topics.

stories.headline: Why is stock market rising today? Sensex surges over 600 points, Nifty above 24,350. 6 key factors Indian markets extended their rally on Thursday, driven by optimism over easing US-Iran tensions and softer oil prices, which lifted investor sentiment.
stories.summary: Indian stock markets extended their rally, with the Sensex surging over 600 points and Nifty above 24,350. The rally is driven by optimism over easing US-Iran tensions and softer oil prices, lifting investor sentiment. This follows a strong performance on Wednesday, where the Sensex jumped 1,264 points and Nifty surged 1.6% due to the US-Iran ceasefire. The markets have been supported by lower oil prices, positive global cues, and buying in financial stocks. However, the overall trend depends on global developments, particularly the outcome of US-Iran talks and crude oil price movements. The recent gains have defied escalating tensions between Israel and Iran, with the Sensex and Nifty 50 posting strong intraday gains despite global uncertainties. The markets are closely watching global developments to determine the sustainability of the rally.

stories.headline: Markets rally over 1% as US-Iran peace talks hopes drive crude oil below $100 Indian benchmark stock indices, Sensex and Nifty, surged over 1 per cent, driven by optimism surrounding potential US-Iran peace talks and a significant drop in
stories.summary: Indian benchmark stock indices, Sensex and Nifty, rallied over 1% on Wednesday, driven by hopes of renewed US-Iran peace talks and a sharp decline in crude oil prices. Crude oil prices fell below $100 per barrel, easing inflation concerns and boosting investor confidence. The surge in the stock markets mirrored gains in global markets, which were also influenced by the potential diplomatic engagement between the US and Iran. The drop in crude oil prices and the possibility of peace talks contributed to the positive sentiment in the markets, leading to a significant increase in the Sensex and Nifty indices. The rally in the Indian stock markets was a result of the combined effect of global market trends and the decline in crude oil prices, which had a positive impact on investor sentiment.

stories.headline: The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex surged over 1.6% after US–Iran talks optimism boosted sentiment, driving broad-based buying. Falling volatility, strong technical si
stories.summary: The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex surged over 1.6% due to optimism over US-Iran talks, driving broad-based buying and falling volatility. The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70. Financial stocks led the gains, but IT stocks dragged the market. The rally came after US President Donald Trump's comments lifted sentiment, despite concerns over tariffs and geopolitical stability. The Indian share market witnessed a strong recovery, with the benchmark indices rising to multi-month highs after a global sentiment improvement following the Iran-Israel truce. Investors are now focused on the global economy and the impact of US trade tariffs on corporate profits and growth. The market's bullish momentum was supported by strong technical signals and sectoral strength.

stories.headline: Sensex, Nifty outlook for tomorrow: Will stock market sustain momentum? Sensex outlook: The intraday market texture is upward but, for day traders, it would be ideal to buy on intraday dips and sell on rallies, said Shrikant Chouhan
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is expected to sustain momentum, with the Sensex and Nifty outlook appearing positive. The Nifty closed at 23,997.35, up 3.78% or 873.70 points, and Nifty futures were trading at 26,190 points, indicating a potential recovery after a weak spell. The Sensex opened 600 points higher, with banking and aviation stocks leading the sharp recovery. Analysts recommend buying on intraday dips and selling on rallies, with Vaishali Parekh suggesting three intraday stocks for today. The market texture is upward, but traders are advised to be cautious and take advantage of intraday opportunities. The opening upside gap remains unfilled, and the Nifty closed at its highs, suggesting a potential continuation of the upward trend. Overall, the market is expected to remain volatile, with traders advised to be prepared for intraday fluctuations.

stories.headline: GSMA reveals MWC26 Kigali line-up, including first-ever Africa GLOMOs and new industry summits Registration is now open for Africa’s most influential connectivity event which shapes the decisions, deals and ideas that will define the
stories.summary: The GSMA has announced the line-up for MWC26 Kigali, a prominent connectivity event in Africa. This year's event will feature the first-ever Africa Global Mobile Awards (GLOMOs) and new industry summits. Registration is now open for the event, which is expected to shape key decisions, deals, and ideas in the industry. MWC26 Kigali is part of the GSMA's global events, including MWC Barcelona, Shanghai, and Doha. The GSMA is the voice of the mobile industry, convening key players across the connectivity ecosystem at its events. The organization provides a platform for industry leaders to share knowledge, showcase innovations, and discuss the latest trends and developments. MWC26 Kigali aims to bring together experts and thought leaders to explore the future of connectivity in Africa and beyond. The event will feature keynotes, exhibitors, and thought leaders from the mobile industry.

stories.headline: Airbus’ autonomous supply-helicopter effort may pave the way for an armed model An automated perception test involved technology from Shield AI, L3 Harris, and Parry Labs.
stories.summary: Airbus is developing an unmanned helicopter concept for Marine Corps logistics missions, which may lead to an armed variant. The company has partnered with Shield AI to integrate its Hivemind autonomous flight software for the Aerial Logistics Connector (ALC) program. Recent tests, including an automated perception test, have been successful, with participation from Shield AI, L3 Harris, and Parry Labs. The tests involved autonomous flight with a DT25 target drone and demonstrated AI-powered systems to defeat unmanned threats. Shield AI has also secured a $2 billion investment to expand its autonomous flight capabilities. The ALC program aims to develop an autonomous supply helicopter, and the technology developed through this program could potentially be used for an armed model in the future. The partnership and testing are ongoing, with footage released of the second autonomous test flight.

stories.headline: A Look At Oklo (OKLO) Valuation As New Nuclear-Focused Board Strengthens Growth Ambitions Oklo (OKLO) is reshaping its leadership by adding four experienced directors and a Lead Independent Director, and by shifting its Chief Technology Officer into
stories.summary: Oklo Inc. has expanded its Board of Directors to 11 members, adding Dr. Mark Peters, David Christian, Derek Kan, and David Park, effective April 10, 2026. Michael Thompson has been appointed as the Lead Independent Director. The new appointments aim to strengthen Oklo's growth ambitions in the nuclear energy sector. Oklo, a designer of advanced nuclear reactors, focuses on fast-fission power plants and is based in Santa Clara, California. The company was founded in 2013 by Jacob and Caroline DeWitte. The new board members bring experience and expertise to support Oklo's goals, despite the company's concept being untested at a commercial scale and operating in a heavily regulated industry. The expansion of the board is seen as a positive step towards Oklo's growth and development in the nuclear energy sector.

stories.headline: Late market roundup: FTSE 100 edges lower amid cautious sentiment London’s blue chip index posted a modest decline on Wednesday, as investors across the globe await progress on potential f
stories.summary: The FTSE 100 index closed down 3.36 points at 9,223.32 on Wednesday, amid cautious sentiment and a rise in oil prices. The increase in oil prices was driven by US-Israeli strikes on Iranian facilities, with Brent oil quoted at $108.21 a barrel. The FTSE 250 ended 98.42 points higher at 21,695.35, while the AIM All-Share closed up 3.72 points at 781.25. The Cboe UK 100 was down 0.2% at 1,055.35, and the Cboe UK 250 was 0.2% lower at 19,372.73. Investors are awaiting the US interest rate decision, which will be announced shortly. The producer price index for final demand increased 0.7% month-on-month in February, adding to concerns about inflation. London's blue chip index underperformed against its peers in Paris and Frankfurt, amid fresh concerns about UK inflation and the economy.

stories.headline: SoftBank lenders ask more banks to join $40 billion OpenAI loan
stories.summary: SoftBank Group Corp. has taken out a $40 billion unsecured loan to fund its $30 billion investment in OpenAI. The loan has a 12-month repayment deadline, and lenders are now inviting more banks to join. This move is a significant test of creditor sentiment towards SoftBank's debt-fueled expansion into artificial intelligence. OpenAI is reportedly seeking $40 billion in a fresh funding round, which would increase its valuation to over $300 billion. The funding round includes $55 billion in conditional or deferred capital, with Amazon's $35 billion contingent on an IPO or AGI milestone, and SoftBank's $20 billion to be provided in quarterly tranches over time. The loan and funding round are crucial for SoftBank's investment in OpenAI, and the outcome will be closely watched by creditors and investors.

stories.headline: Google Unveils PaLM 2 AI Model with Improved Performance and Efficiency
stories.summary: Google introduced PaLM 2, a new language model with improved multilingual, reasoning, and coding capabilities, at I/O 2023. PaLM 2 has been trained in over 100 languages, enabling it to understand, generate, and translate nuanced text, including idioms and poems. This enhanced version of the original PaLM model is scalable and generalizable across tasks such as conversing, reasoning, and creating. PaLM 2 will be used in Google's updated Bard chat tool, competing with OpenAI's ChatGPT, and will serve as the foundational model for new AI features. Developers can access PaLM 2 through Google's PaLM API, Firebase, and Colab. The model's improved performance and efficiency will support various applications, including generative AI services announced at Google I/O 2023. PaLM 2's capabilities will be integrated into multiple Google services, further expanding the company's AI offerings.

stories.headline: Microsoft Expands Azure OpenAI Service to More Developers and Businesses
stories.summary: Microsoft has expanded its Azure OpenAI service to more developers and businesses. The service is constantly evolving with new features and models being released regularly. Recent updates include new models designed to help developers build AI applications for text, image, audio, and video faster and at lower cost. The Microsoft Agent Framework, an open-source SDK for coordinating multiple AI agents, is now available, along with OpenAI's new Agent SDK. Additionally, OpenAI has contracted to purchase $250B of Azure services, but Microsoft will no longer have a right of first refusal to be OpenAI's compute provider. This allows OpenAI to provide API access to US government national security customers, regardless of the cloud provider. The expansion is part of Microsoft's efforts to position Azure at the center of global AI innovation and enterprise adoption.

stories.headline: Meta Releases Llama AI Model for Research and Commercial Applications
stories.summary: Meta has released its Llama AI model for research and commercial applications, breaking away from the traditional closed AI approach. The Llama 4 model has three variants: Scout, Maverick, and the upcoming Behemoth, offering flexibility for enterprise deployments and startup innovation. Unlike other models, Llama is open-source, allowing developers to download and customize it freely. Meta unveiled the Llama 4 models at its inaugural AI developer conference, LlamaCon, in April 2025, and has integrated them into its platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, and WhatsApp, without introducing a paywall. The company has also released open-source tools to support the model's use, enabling developers to explore, build, and defend Llama models more easily. The release marks a significant milestone in Meta's AI journey, combining open-source releases with platform integration.
